name: Oktapodi Toursi (Pickled Octopus)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare and wash the octopus as described in "Octopus in Red Wine." Place the head and tentacles in a pan with 6-8 tablespoons water. Cover and simmer for 60 to 75 minutes, or until tender. Test with a skewer.
2. Drain any remaining liquid from the octopus and let it cool.
3. Cut the flesh into 1/2-inch strips and pack them loosely into a screw-topped jar.
4. Mix enough olive oil and red wine vinegar to fill the jar, adding more of either ingredient as needed to fully cover the seafood. Stir in minced garlic and season with salt and pepper. If using dried thyme, mix it with the liquid at this stage.
5. Pour the mixture over the octopus pieces, ensuring every piece is completely immersed. If using thyme stalks, place them into the jar.
6. Cover the jar tightly and let it stand for at least 4-5 days before serving.
7. To serve, drain the octopus and arrange on individual plates or saucers with lemon wedges. Serve with cubes of bread speared on cocktail sticks.
